preamble: The shift from [g] to [o] is a variant of velar vocalization where the consonant weakens into a back rounded mid-vowel, often forming a diphthong with the preceding vowel.

examples.note: OE intervocalic [ɣ] vocalized to contribute to the [oʊ] diphthong.

sources: Baugh, A. C. & Cable, T. (2013). A History of the English Language (6th ed.). Routledge.
